diagrams.title: System Call Transition: User Mode to Kernel Mode
diagrams.description: Shows the CPU privilege level transition when a user process makes a system call. The process moves from User Mode (Ring 3) to Kernel Mode (Ring 0) through a software interrupt or syscall instruction.
diagrams.purpose: Illustrates how system calls provide controlled access to privileged kernel operations while maintaining system security

diagrams.title: File Descriptor Inheritance on Fork
diagrams.description: Shows how file descriptors are duplicated (not shared) when fork() is called. Both parent and child have their own descriptor tables pointing to the same open file entries in the system-wide file table.
elements: Each fd points to same file description
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates that fork() creates independent file descriptor tables but underlying file descriptions are shared between parent and child

diagrams.title: Process States and System Calls
diagrams.description: Shows the process state transitions and which system calls trigger them: new, ready, running, blocked (waiting), terminated.
diagrams.purpose: Helps visualize how processes move between states and understand when system calls cause state transitions
